claim: A Cardan-grille generator (Rugg 2004 model) operating on a closed symbol inventory cannot reproduce Hand A's categorical 0/9784 n-on-consonant-inner exclusion gap at N>=2000 attachment opportunities with greater than 1% probability across plausible grille configurations. Confirmation would establish that the n/C-inner gap requires a grammatical constraint and cannot be mechanically manufactured by grille-based text generation — a direct refutation of the strongest 'meaningless-text' alternative to Brain-V's natural-language/scribal hypotheses.
evidence_for: H-BV-CARBON-SAMPLE-VALIDATION-01 and H-BV-HAND-B-SUBCLASS-01 together established that n-exclusivity holds on 9784 consonant-inner tokens across five independent samples (full Hand A, full Hand B, f8, f26, f47, f68). Under independent-sampling assumptions, P(0 instances) = (1-p)^9784 where p = P(n | random outer) ~ 0.25; this is ~10^-1200 i.e. effectively impossible by chance.

Rugg's grille proposal explicitly requires the generated text to exhibit NO grammatical constraints — the apparent regularities should arise from grille geometry. If a constraint as strong as the n/C exclusion CANNOT be produced by any plausible grille, Rugg's proposal cannot explain Brain-V's finding.

The n/C exclusion is not a frequency dip but a categorical ban. Categorical bans in grille-generated text would require specific grille geometries that forbid the corresponding cells; they do not arise naturally from random cell arrangements.
evidence_against: A carefully-constructed grille CAN produce categorical cell exclusions by design (e.g., a grille with holes that never expose a specific (inner, outer) combination). The test is whether RANDOM / PLAUSIBLE grille configurations reproduce the gap, not whether ANY grille could be constructed to do so.

Rugg's 2004 paper describes specific grille parameters (cell sizes, movement patterns, syllable tables). Without replicating Rugg's exact model, the simulation tests an abstracted grille concept — results apply to the concept, not to Rugg's specific 2004 instantiation.
The simulation uses independent glyph sampling from Hand A frequency distributions; real scribal practice (whether grille-based or not) may include additional structure not captured here.
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE.

  GRILLE MODEL (abstracted):
    A grille generator is modelled as a 10x5 boolean ALLOW-MATRIX over (Layer-1 inner) x (Layer-2 outer), plus a stem-generation component. The generator produces tokens by:
      1. Sample a stem of random length 1-3 glyph-units uniformly.
      2. Sample a Layer-1 inner weighted by Hand A's observed inner-position frequencies.
      3. Sample a Layer-2 outer weighted by Hand A's observed outer-position frequencies. If the (inner, outer) cell is FORBIDDEN by the allow-matrix, reject and resample the outer.
      4. If all outers for a given inner are forbidden, resample the inner.
    Each generator configuration has a FORBIDDEN-DENSITY parameter k: each of the 50 cells is independently forbidden with probability k.

  GRILLE CONFIGURATIONS: Run 200 simulations each at FORBIDDEN-DENSITY k in {0.0, 0.10, 0.20, 0.30, 0.50}. Total: 1000 simulations.
    Rationale: k=0.0 is pure independent sampling (no grille constraint); k=0.5 is a heavily-constrained grille (half the cells forbidden); intermediate values sweep plausible grille designs.

  PER-SIMULATION:
    Generate 11022 tokens (matches Hand A's raw corpus size including stem-only tokens).
    Filter to N>=3 glyph-unit tokens.
    Compute the 10-inner x 5-outer contingency table.
    Partition inners into VOWEL-class {i,e,o,a} and CONSONANT-class {ch,d,k,t,sh,l} (Hand A's locked partition).

  DETECTION CRITERION:
    A simulation is deemed to REPRODUCE the n/C-inner gap if:
      (i) count(n as outer | inner in CONSONANT-class) == 0, AND
      (ii) count(inner in CONSONANT-class) >= 2000.
    Report the count and rate of reproducing simulations, overall and per-k.

  DECISION RULE (LOCKED):
    CONFIRMED (Rugg-grille REFUTED for Hand A): <1% of 1000 simulations reproduce the gap. The n/C-inner gap requires a grammatical constraint.
    MARGINAL: 1-10% of simulations reproduce. The gap is achievable by grille but rare.
    REFUTED (Brain-V finding not diagnostic): >10% of simulations reproduce. The categorical gap could arise from mechanical generation and is not evidence for grammar.

  SANITY CHECK (non-decision-feeding): at k=0.0 (no forbidden cells), reproduction rate should be near-zero. At k=1.0 (all cells forbidden — no generation possible), generation fails entirely. Intermediate k values sweep the space of plausible grille designs.
test_metric: fraction of 1000 simulations where count(n | C-inner) = 0 AND count(C-inner) >= 2000
test_threshold: CONFIRMED if <1% reproduce. MARGINAL if 1-10%. REFUTED if >10%.
reasoning: Brain-V's 15th-c. synthesis flagged Rugg's Cardan-grille hoax model (2004) as the strongest 'meaningless-text' alternative that could potentially reproduce closed-inventory and categorical-gap findings by mechanical construction. The Rugg hypothesis, if viable, would make Brain-V's structural findings non-diagnostic for natural-language status. This test directly attacks the Rugg hypothesis by simulating 1000 grille configurations and measuring how often they reproduce the observable gap. A <1% reproduction rate would effectively rule out random/plausible grille generation as an explanation, shifting posterior weight toward grammatical explanations (candidates (a) Latin scribal abbreviation, (b) constructed script, or (c) agglutinative language). Note: the test addresses ABSTRACTED grille behavior; it does not refute Rugg's specific 2004 instantiation, which may include additional machinery not modelled here.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-17 user-directed grille-null test, part of 3-test batch (CAPPELLI-MATCH-01, RUGG-GRILLE-01, AGGLUTINATIVE-SCREEN-01)
